title: A Study of Android Application Security
authors: William Enck; Damien Octeau; Patrick McDaniel; Swarat Chaudhuri
abstract_brief: Static analysis of 1100 Android apps showing heavy use of device identifiers and deep integration of ad and analytics code.

title: SUPOR: Precise and Scalable Sensitive User Input Detection for Android Apps
authors: Jianjun Huang; Zhichun Li; Xusheng Xiao; Zhenyu Wu; Kangjie Lu; Xiangyu Zhang; Guofei Jiang
abstract_brief: Detects credential, finance, and medical input fields in Android apps and supports downstream privacy-flow analysis from user-entered sensitive data.

title: Detecting Passive Content Leaks and Pollution in Android Applications
authors: Yajin Zhou; Xuxian Jiang
abstract_brief: Shows how open content providers leak or permit tampering of private in-app data and quantifies prevalence at scale.

title: Execute This! Analyzing Unsafe and Malicious Dynamic Code Loading in Android Applications
authors: Sebastian Poeplau; Yanick Fratantonio; Antonio Bianchi; Christopher Kruegel; Giovanni Vigna
abstract_brief: Studies runtime code loading as both an evasion technique and a genuine security weakness in benign Android apps.

title: AppSealer: Automatic Generation of Vulnerability-Specific Patches for Preventing Component Hijacking Attacks in Android Applications
authors: Mu Zhang; Heng Yin
abstract_brief: Targets Android component hijacking and is useful for exported component, permission, and trust-boundary rule extraction.

title: Why Eve and Mallory Love Android: An Analysis of Android SSL (in)Security
authors: Sascha Fahl; Marian Harbach; Thomas Muders; Matthew Smith; Lars Baumgartner; Bernd Freisleben
abstract_brief: Foundational Android TLS misuse study covering weak certificate validation and MITM-prone custom SSL logic.

title: Measuring the Insecurity of Mobile Deep Links of Android
authors: Fang Liu; Chun Wang; Andres Pico; Danfeng Yao; Gang Wang
abstract_brief: Empirical study of scheme URLs, App Links, and Intent URLs showing frequent misconfiguration and link hijacking risk.

title: Iframes/Popups Are Dangerous in Mobile WebView: Studying and Mitigating Differential Context Vulnerabilities
authors: Guangliang Yang; Jeff Huang; Guofei Gu
abstract_brief: Shows how iframe and popup behavior inside Android WebView can break isolation, integrity, and permission expectations.

title: Cross-Boundary Mobile Tracking: Exploring Java-to-JavaScript Information Diffusion in WebViews
authors: Sohom Datta; Michalis Diamantaris; Ahsan Zafar; Junhua Su; Anupam Das; Jason Polakis; Alexandros Kapravelos
abstract_brief: Tracks privacy-relevant data moving from Android Java code into WebView JavaScript and then to web endpoints.

title: PolicyLint: Investigating Internal Privacy Policy Contradictions on Google Play
authors: Benjamin Andow; Samin Yaseer Mahmud; Wenyu Wang; Justin Whitaker; William Enck; Bradley Reaves; Kapil Singh; Tao Xie
abstract_brief: Analyzes privacy policy contradictions and supports rules about inconsistent or misleading disclosures.

title: A Fait Accompli? An Empirical Study into the Absence of Consent to Third-Party Tracking in Android Apps
authors: Konrad Kollnig; Reuben Binns; Pierre Dewitte; Max Van Kleek; Ge Wang; Daniel Omeiza; Helena Webb; Nigel Shadbolt
abstract_brief: Shows that many Android apps engage in third-party tracking before obtaining meaningful consent.

title: What’s Done Is Not What’s Claimed: Detecting and Interpreting Inconsistencies in App Behaviors
authors: Chang Yue; Kai Chen; Zhixiu Guo; Jun Dai; Xiaoyan Sun; Yi Yang
abstract_brief: Extracts user-facing behavior summaries and highlights inconsistencies between expected app purpose and actual sensitive operations.

title: Security Concerns in Android mHealth Apps
authors: Dongjing He; Muhammad Naveed; Carl A Gunter; Klara Nahrstedt
abstract_brief: Systematic mHealth study covering insecure Internet communication, third-party servers, Bluetooth, logs, and external storage.

title: Unaddressed privacy risks in accredited health and wellness apps: a cross-sectional systematic assessment
authors: Kit Huckvale; Jose Tomas Prieto; Myra Tilney; Pierre-Jean Benghozi; Josip Car
abstract_brief: Protocol-based testing of accredited health apps found local storage without encryption and frequent unencrypted transmission of personal data.

title: Exploring the Far Side of Mobile Health: Information Security and Privacy of Mobile Health Apps on iOS and Android
authors: Tobias Dehling; Fangjian Gao; Stephan Schneider; Ali Sunyaev
abstract_brief: Builds a broad damage-oriented framework for assessing privacy and security exposure across health app archetypes.

title: Assessment of the Data Sharing and Privacy Practices of Smartphone Apps for Depression and Smoking Cessation
authors: Kit Huckvale; John Torous; Mark E Larsen
abstract_brief: Compares privacy disclosures to observed transmissions in mental health and smoking cessation apps.

title: Mobile health and privacy: cross sectional study
authors: Gioacchino Tangari; Muhammad Ikram; Kiran Ijaz; Mohamed Ali Kaafar; Shlomo Berkovsky
abstract_brief: Combines large-scale static code analysis, traffic analysis, third-party mapping, and privacy policy comparison for mHealth apps.

title: Analyzing security issues of android mobile health and medical applications
authors: Gioacchino Tangari; Muhammad Ikram; I Wayan Budi Sentana; Kiran Ijaz; Mohamed Ali Kaafar; Shlomo Berkovsky
abstract_brief: Large-scale Android mHealth analysis covering permissions, dangerous APIs, traffic, malware detection signals, and certificate security.

title: A Study of the Feasibility of Co-located App Attacks against BLE and a Large-Scale Analysis of the Current Application-Layer Security Landscape
authors: Pallavi Sivakumaran; Jorge Blasco
abstract_brief: Shows that pairing-protected BLE data is often still accessible to unauthorized co-located Android apps and that app-layer crypto is frequently missing.

title: Who’s Accessing My Data? Application-Level Access Control for Bluetooth Low Energy
authors: Pallavi Sivakumaran; Jorge Blasco
abstract_brief: Extends the BLE threat model from device-level pairing to application-level authorization on multi-app platforms like Android phones.

title: Inside Job: Understanding and Mitigating the Threat of External Device Mis-Bonding on Android
authors: Muhammad Naveed; Xiaoyong Zhou; Soteris Demetriou; XiaoFeng Wang; Carl A Gunter
abstract_brief: Shows that Bluetooth permission on Android can let unauthorized apps interact with bonded external devices and inject or read data.

title: Privacy Settings of Third-Party Libraries in Android Apps: A Study of Facebook SDKs
authors: David Rodriguez; Joseph A Calandrino; Jose M Del Alamo; Norman Sadeh
abstract_brief: Studies privacy-related settings and defaults of Facebook SDKs and compares app configuration choices with disclosed practices.

title: Your Signal, Their Data: An Empirical Privacy Analysis of Wireless-scanning SDKs in Android
authors: Aniketh Girish; Joel Reardon; Juan Tapiador; Srdjan Matic; Narseo Vallina-Rodriguez
abstract_brief: Analyzes commercial SDKs that use BLE and Wi-Fi scanning as a proxy for location and continuous profiling.

title: Panoptispy: Characterizing Audio and Video Exfiltration from Android Applications
authors: Elleen Pan; Jingjing Ren; Martina Lindorfer; Christo Wilson; David Choffnes
abstract_brief: Large-scale study of media permissions and unexpected exfiltration of screenshots, images, audio, and video from Android apps.
